item.title: Excited-State Orbital Angular Momentum Enables All-Optical Molecular Spin Coherence
item.description:
Chem. Sci., 2026, Accepted Manuscript
DOI: 10.1039/D6SC04497B, Edge Article
Open Access
  This article is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported Licence.
Erica Sutcliffe, Jonathan P Aalto, Ryan G Hadt
Paramagnetic molecules are promising quantum sensors with dimensions and environmental compatibility inaccessible to solid-state defects. Realizing this promise, however, requires optical methods for initializing and reading out coherent spin dynamics....
The content of this RSS Feed (c) The Royal Society of Chemistry

item.title: Orthogonal Nanopores Cross-Validation for Multiplex Single-Molecule Profiling
item.description:
Chem. Sci., 2026, Advance Article
DOI: 10.1039/D6SC03524H, Edge Article
Open Access
  This article is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 3.0 Unported Licence.
Lin-Lin Zhang, Fan Gao, Yi-He Wei, Cheng-Bing Zhong, Bingqing Xia, Liuqing Wen, Yi-Tao Long, Yi-Lun Ying
A multiplex nanopore sensor integrating orthogonal nanopores was developed for cross-validated single-molecule identification and relative quantification of diverse biomolecules, providing a new strategy for multiplexed single-molecule profiling.
To cite this article before page numbers are assigned, use the DOI form of citation above.
The content of this RSS Feed (c) The Royal Society of Chemistry

item.title: Manipulating Excited-State Dynamics Through Macrocycle Positioning in a Rotaxane for Sensitive and Discriminative Methanol Sensing
item.description:
Chem. Sci., 2026, 17,12039-12046
DOI: 10.1039/D6SC00753H, Edge Article
Open Access
  This article is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 3.0 Unported Licence.
Yu Wang, Yalei Ma, Ruijuan Wen, Jing Li, Taihong Liu, Liping Ding, Rong Miao, Yu Fang
A supramolecular strategy for excited-state dynamics manipulation is demonstrated by controlling the distance between the macrocycle and the fluorophore within the rotaxane, which enables sensitive and discriminative methanol vapor sensing.
The content of this RSS Feed (c) The Royal Society of Chemistry
